name: Paperclip
description: Control plane for autonomous AI companies — manages AI agents as employees with org structure, budgets, goals, and governance.

description.value: Open-source control plane that orchestrates fleets of AI coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Cursor, OpenClaw, Bash, HTTP) as a company — org chart, budgets, goals, governance, and ticketed work.

creator.comment: GitHub org is paperclipai; primary author on recent commits is Mirco Dotta (@dotta).

first-release.comment: Repo created 2026-03-02; earliest tagged release v0.3.0 on 2026-03-09.

latest-release.comment: v2026.416.0 released 2026-04-16; calendar-versioned with frequent cuts.

pricing-model.comment: Open source, self-hosted, no Paperclip account required. No hosted paid tier offered.

covered-phases.comment: Work is represented as tickets (tasks), executed by agents (implementation), and gated by Agent Reviews + human approvals (review). No distinct spec, test, or deployment phase.

methodology-style.comment: Coordinates distinct agents with different roles (multi-agent); value is the orchestration harness (org chart, heartbeats, budgets, tickets) rather than a prescribed coding process.

opinionated.comment: Strongly prescribes its company model (org chart, roles, heartbeats, budgets, governance). Not opinionated about the coding workflow itself.

ceremony-level.comment: Requires defining companies, org charts, agent roles, budgets, and approvals — more than lightweight, but not a heavy documentation/gate load.

mechanization-level.comment: Single `npx paperclipai onboard` command sets up the full system; agents run on scheduled heartbeats once configured. Not managed-platform (self-hosted), so not very-high.

hitl-checkpoints.comment: Explicit approval gates for hiring and strategy; Agent Reviews and Approvals shipped.

approval-gates.comment: Strategy/hiring approvals map to plan; Agent Reviews gate implementation. No deploy phase.

autonomous-e2e.comment: Once configured, agents run autonomously 24/7 on heartbeats; pitched as managing autonomous businesses from a phone.

autonomy-level.comment: Default config runs agents autonomously on heartbeats with selective human approvals (hiring, strategy, agent reviews). Not fully hands-off because governance gates fire at configured points.

rollback-support.comment: README: 'config changes are revisioned, and bad changes can be rolled back safely.' Humans can pause or terminate any agent at any time.

supported-agents.comment: Also supports OpenClaw, Bash, and HTTP-compatible agents — mapped to 'other'. Any runtime that can receive a heartbeat.

editor-integrations.comment: React UI dashboard plus CLI installer; mobile-responsive. No editor-specific integrations.

model-agnostic.comment: Explicitly agent- and model-agnostic: 'Any agent, any runtime, one org chart.'

mcp-support.comment: No mention of Model Context Protocol in README, landing page, or docs as of 2026-04-21.

artifact-formats.comment: Agent configuration via AGENTS.md and SKILLS.md (markdown). Work artifacts (tickets, audit logs, org charts) are stored in Postgres rather than as files on disk.

artifacts-covered.comment: Tickets = tasks; immutable audit log = change-logs; approval/governance trail = decisions. Dedicated 'Artifacts & Work Products' on roadmap but not yet shipped.

project-layout.value: Server-side Postgres stores tickets, org charts, and audit logs. Per-project AGENTS.md and SKILLS.md drive agent configuration. Company exports via companies.sh with secret scrubbing and collision handling.

artifacts-in-git.comment: Primary artifacts (tickets, audit logs, org charts, budgets) live in the embedded Postgres database. AGENTS.md/SKILLS.md configs may be versioned in the target project's repo, but the workflow's own artifacts are not.

language-agnostic.comment: Coordinates agents regardless of what they code in; orchestration layer makes no assumption about target stack.

enforcement-mechanisms.comment: Governance via manual approval gates for hiring/strategy and via Agent Reviews for work output. Budget limits also enforce mechanically but fall outside the listed tag set.

strict-phase-ordering.comment: Ticket-based work model, not a strict phase pipeline; tickets can be created, delegated, and worked in any order within the org chart.

artifact-validation.comment: Ticket/org schemas are enforced by the DB, but agent-produced content within tickets is free-form text and not schema-validated.

memory-implementation.comment: Task state, ticket threads, and audit logs persisted in Postgres; agent context and skill discovery via markdown AGENTS.md / SKILLS.md files. Memory/Knowledge is a roadmap item for richer memory.

persistent-memory.comment: 'Sessions persist across reboots'; agents resume the same task context across heartbeats.

project-context-files.comment: Uses AGENTS.md and SKILLS.md as project-scoped context files.

cost-tracking.comment: Tool-call level tracing includes token/cost data; monthly per-agent budgets throttle agents when exhausted.

run-history.comment: 'Every conversation traced. Every decision explained. Full tool-call tracing and immutable audit log.'

telemetry-opt.comment: Anonymous telemetry is disclosed; opt-out via PAPERCLIP_TELEMETRY_DISABLED=1, DO_NOT_TRACK=1, CI=true, or telemetry.enabled: false.

custom-templates.comment: Companies are exportable/importable via companies.sh; SKILLS.md and AGENTS.md are user-authored templates. Clipmart template marketplace is 'coming soon'.

hooks.comment: Plugin system shipped — plugins can add knowledge bases, tracing, queues, etc. Scheduled heartbeats and event-triggered execution provide lifecycle entry points.

sub-agents.comment: Skills Manager ships; org chart supports custom roles, titles, and reporting lines; SKILLS.md injects runtime skills into agents.

marketplace.comment: Community plugin list at awesome-paperclip. Clipmart (branded in-app marketplace) is announced but not yet shipped.

install-mechanism.comment: Primary install: `npx paperclipai onboard --yes`. Manual alternative is git clone + pnpm install.

setup-time.comment: Quickstart targets a single command with interactive setup for DB/auth/first company. Requires Node.js 20+ and pnpm 9.15+ already installed.

self-hosted.comment: Runs locally as a Node.js server with embedded Postgres, or self-deploys to user-controlled infra.

git-worktree-support.comment: No mention of git worktrees in README or docs as of 2026-04-21; worktree parallelization is delegated to the underlying coding agents.

documentation-quality.comment: Docs site, quickstart, FAQ, development guide, roadmap, and contributing guide. Some roadmap features are aspirational but shipped-feature docs are solid.

github-stars.comment: Star count via GitHub API on 2026-04-21. Project launched March 2026; star count still growing very quickly.

active-maintenance.comment: Repo pushed_at 2026-04-21; release v2026.416.0 cut 2026-04-16; daily commit cadence.

example-projects.comment: Template categories are listed (Content Marketing Agency, Crypto Trading Desk, E-commerce Operator, YouTube Factory, Dev Agency, Real Estate Leads) but Clipmart is 'coming soon' and no fully documented case studies are published as of 2026-04-21.

community-channel.comment: Public Discord invite plus GitHub Issues and GitHub Discussions.
